summary:
The information doesn’t clearly indicate which if any of the mentioned hormones are inhibited at a hypothalamic level; therefore, based strictly on what’s stated here neither prolactin nor growth hormone can conclusively be said to have such effects alone among their roles described so far. Agent2 concluded with \(\boxed{\text{None}}\) since both were considered individually insufficient for proof within the constraints presented initially regarding clear hypthathalmic-inhibiting actions specified per typical medical criteria discussed earlier during analysis steps outlined above. 

Note: I've adjusted my response significantly from Round 1 while still adhering closely to your instructions about focusing just on critical aspects related to answering 'which' type questions accurately concerning hormonal regulation pathways involving feedback loops initiated centrally rather than peripheral influences detailed elsewhere outside our scope today!
